text: EU EXTENDS IRAN SANCTIONS: 262 individuals + 53 entities hit with travel bans and asset freezes through April 2027; DOE issues 10M barrel emergency SPR exchange RFP

DAY 33 EVE: Gas $4.06 (AAA); Brent closing ~$101/WTI ~$103; Trump address 9PM ET tonight; Kharazi struck; Pezeshkian open letter; EU sanctions; 35-nation Hormuz meeting tomorrow; IEA 'April worse than March'; war premium fading on peace signals

IDF ELIMINATES IRGC QUDS FORCE ENGINEERING CHIEF: Mahdi Vafaei, head of Quds Force's Lebanon Corps engineering — responsible for Hezbollah tunnels for 20 years — killed in Israeli airstrike on Mahallat, Iran

TRUMP PRIME-TIME ADDRESS: Operation Epic Fury 'nearing completion' — war could end in 2-3 weeks; sets APRIL 6 DEADLINE for Iran to reopen Hormuz or face destruction of energy infrastructure including Kharg Island oil facilities

NETANYAHU PASSOVER 'TEN PLAGUES' SPEECH: Claims Israel inflicted 'ten plagues on Iran's axis of evil' — says nuclear and missile capability destroyed, Iran's trillion-dollar investment 'gone down the drain'
IRAN DENIES CEASEFIRE REQUEST: FM spokesman calls Trump's claim Iran 'begged' for ceasefire 'false and baseless' — Araghchi confirms message exchanges via Pakistan but says 'trust level at zero'

OIL SPIKES ON TRUMP SPEECH: Brent surges to $106.42 (+5%); WTI $104.21 (+4.1%) after Trump vows to hit Iran 'extremely hard' — reverses earlier dip on peace hopes; April 6 energy deadline fears grip markets

DAY 33 NIGHT: Trump address sets April 6 deadline; Netanyahu 'Ten Plagues'; Iran denies ceasefire; Brent $106/WTI $104 spiking; Vafaei + Hajj Sadiq eliminated; 46 Hezbollah waves; IDF 400+ strikes; gas $4.06; UK Hormuz conference tomorrow

IRAN OVERNIGHT SALVOS: 4 ballistic missile barrages since midnight — cluster warheads strike Bnei Brak wounding 4 including 2 BABIES; sewage pipes damaged; impact sites in Tel Aviv city center; Passover under fire
HEZBOLLAH PASSOVER BARRAGE: 50+ rockets at northern Israel; 2 wounded in Kiryat Shmona, 3 in Nahariya, 1 in Kabri; rocket hit building in Shfaram; hundreds of thousands forced into shelters
OIL SURGES 8%: Brent $109.16 (+8.0%), WTI $108.36 (+8.2%) — Trump escalation vow reverses April 1 peace dip; Asia markets plunge (Nikkei -1.4%, Kospi -2.82%); gas now $4.064/gal (AAA)

sources.name: CNBC — Oil surges on Trump speech

text: IRGC THREATENS 18 US TECH GIANTS: Apple, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing listed as targets — orders employee evacuations; unprecedented corporate infrastructure targeting

UK 35-NATION HORMUZ SUMMIT TODAY: FM Cooper chairing virtual meeting (France, Germany, Italy, Canada, UAE, Japan) — US NOT attending; goal: restore Strait freedom of navigation; military planners to follow

CHINA-PAKISTAN 5-POINT PEACE PLAN: First major power ceasefire initiative — immediate ceasefire, humanitarian access, stop infrastructure attacks, restore Hormuz shipping, comprehensive peace framework under UN Charter

DAY 34 AM: Brent $109/WTI $108 (surging +8%); Iran 4 salvos overnight (cluster warheads, 4 hurt); Hezbollah 50+ rockets; IRGC threatens tech companies; UK 35-nation Hormuz summit; China-Pakistan peace plan; gas $4.064; Rubio-Hegseth ground troop feud; Argentina designates IRGC terrorist

PASTEUR INSTITUTE STRUCK: US-Israeli airstrike destroys century-old medical research center in Tehran — Iran's Health Ministry: 'barbaric assault on international health security'; 316 healthcare centers damaged since Feb 28
US EMBASSY BAGHDAD: IMMINENT MILITIA ATTACKS WITHIN 24-48 HOURS — US citizens told to leave Iraq immediately; airspace closed; targets include diplomatic facilities, energy infrastructure, hotels

UK HORMUZ SUMMIT: 40 nations attend (expanded from 35) — signed statement demanding Iran reopen strait; US notably absent; follow-up military planning pledged; Iran-Oman separately finalize draft navigation regime
GUTERRES: 'EDGE OF WIDER WAR' — UN chief directly addresses US/Israel: 'high time to stop the war'; dispatches envoy Jean Arnault to region; Macron calls Hormuz military op 'unrealistic'

text: DAY 34 PM: Brent $109/WTI $111 (volatile +8-13%); gas $4.08 (AAA); Pasteur Institute struck; Baghdad militia warning; 40-nation Hormuz summit; Iran-Oman navigation draft; Guterres 'edge of wider war'; 2 IDF killed in Lebanon; stocks S&P -0.81%; gold plunges -2.7%

HEGSETH FIRES ARMY CHIEF: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George effective immediately — replaced by military aide Gen. LaNeve; major leadership upheaval during active wartime operations
